_comment: Large map fixtures for GDALKit, too big to commit to git. Download on demand for manual / opt-in integration testing. Each entry: what the file is (description), where to get it (url), and the GDALKit edge case it exercises (significance) — usually something we had to fix. The small fixtures actually committed in this folder are RGB.byte.tif and paletted.tif.

fixtures.name: usgs-drg-c41078a1
fixtures.description: USGS Digital Raster Graphic (DRG) topo quad, scanned/paletted GeoTIFF, 7202x5593, WGS 84 / UTM zone 17N (EPSG:32617).
fixtures.significance: Paletted: one Byte band + a 256-entry RGB colour table. The warp kept the palette band, so the renderer read palette *indices* as grayscale and the map came out BLACK. Fixed by expanding colour-table sources to RGB via an in-memory VRT before warping (GDALKit 0.1.4). The canonical regression file for #19.

fixtures.name: usgs-drg-o41078a1
fixtures.description: Companion USGS DRG quad to c41078a1, paletted GeoTIFF, 5032x6793, UTM zone 17N.
fixtures.significance: Also paletted (colour table) — same black-render bug as c41078a1, and the second sample in the #19 report. Use it to confirm the palette-expand fix holds across more than one DRG.

fixtures.name: ustopo-tx-7-l-ranch
fixtures.description: USGS US Topo GeoPDF — '7 L Ranch, TX' 7.5-minute quad (West Texas near Monahans), 3600x4350 page, NAD83 / UTM zone 13N.
fixtures.significance: GeoPDF whose LPTS `ly` is measured from the TOP of the page; the importer assumed bottom-origin, so the GCP latitude axis was inverted and the map warped UPSIDE-DOWN (collar text reversed). Fixed by enforcing north-up from the GCPs (mirror the line axis when latitude rises with pixel-line). Also exercises the app-side CoreGraphics GeoPDF import path (the iOS GDAL build has no PDF driver). Browse more US Topo quads via https://apps.nationalmap.gov/downloader/ (dataset 'US Topo').

fixtures.name: nsw-hall-8727-4s
fixtures.description: NSW 1:25 000 topographic map 'Hall' (sheet 8727-4S), 2022 edition, collar-on Geospatial PDF, 3248x1672, GDA2020 / MGA zone 55.
fixtures.source: NSW Government — Spatial Services (portal.spatial.nsw.gov.au); © State of New South Wales
fixtures.significance: Properly-georeferenced GeoPDF on the modern GDA2020 datum — exercises the GDA2020 -> EPSG:3857 reproject and the app-side CoreGraphics GeoPDF import path. The 'Hall' sheet from the slow-render (#15) and tile-alignment (#16) discussions. Replaces the legacy Geoscience Australia scan (8727_4_Hall.tif, ~261 MB) which had NO embedded georeferencing (it relied on a sidecar world file). A good Australian-datum companion to the US Topo GeoPDF (#17), with a different LPTS layout to check the north-up orientation logic against.

fixtures.name: ga-act-region-100k
fixtures.description: Geoscience Australia ACT 1:100 000 region map, GeoPDF, ~741 MB.
fixtures.significance: Extreme large-input stress test: a ~741 MB GeoPDF, far over the ~50 MB practical target. Used to scope an upload/size cap (#3) and to check warp-on-load memory behaviour / iOS jetsam against the GDAL_CACHEMAX cap. Likely needs progressive rendering (#15) to be usable.
